register_metadata.title: OSFI Risk Register — Canadian GRC Toolkit (Sample)
register_metadata.description: Sample risk register demonstrating OSFI B-10, B-13, and E-21 aligned risk entries with inherent/residual scoring and automated RAG status.

risks.title: End-of-Life Operating Systems in Production
risks.description: Several production servers are running operating system versions that have reached end-of-life and no longer receive security patches, exposing the institution to known vulnerabilities.
controls: Compensating network segmentation isolating EOL systems from internet-facing zones
Enhanced IDS/IPS monitoring on segments hosting EOL assets
Approved exception process with quarterly CISO sign-off
Migration project initiated with target completion Q3 2026

risks.title: Ransomware Attack on Core Banking Infrastructure
risks.description: Threat actors could deploy ransomware against core banking systems, encrypting data and disrupting operations, potentially triggering OSFI incident reporting obligations under B-13.
controls: Immutable offline backups tested quarterly with documented recovery time objectives
EDR/XDR solution deployed across all endpoints and servers
Network micro-segmentation limiting lateral movement
Privileged access workstations (PAWs) enforced for administrative tasks
Annual tabletop exercise for ransomware scenario response

risks.title: Fourth-Party Risk from Critical Cloud Provider
risks.description: A tier-1 critical third-party vendor relies on a sub-processor for data processing. Failure or security incident at the sub-processor could impact service continuity without the institution having direct contractual oversight.
risks.osfi_reference: OSFI B-10 §4 – Subcontracting and Fourth-Party Risk
controls: Contractual clause requiring vendor to notify institution of material sub-processor changes within 10 business days
Annual third-party risk assessment including sub-processor review
Concentration risk assessment identifying alternative vendors

risks.title: Unauthorized Cross-Border Data Transfer
risks.description: Personal information of Quebec residents may be transferred to jurisdictions lacking equivalent privacy protections, creating regulatory exposure under Quebec Law 25 and PIPEDA breach obligations.
risks.osfi_reference: Quebec Law 25 §17 / PIPEDA – Cross-Border Data Transfer
controls: Data residency OPA policy enforcing approved Canadian regions at deployment
Data classification tagging applied to all personal information repositories
Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) mandatory for new third-party data sharing
Quarterly data flow mapping review with Privacy Officer sign-off

risks.title: Undetected Privileged Access Abuse
risks.description: A malicious or compromised privileged user could exfiltrate sensitive data or make unauthorized configuration changes if privileged session activity is not comprehensively logged and monitored.
risks.osfi_reference: OSFI B-13 §5 – Access Controls / OSFI E-21 §3
controls: Privileged Access Management (PAM) solution enforcing just-in-time access
Privileged session logging policy (OPA) validating required fields on all events
SIEM alerts on anomalous privileged access patterns
Quarterly access recertification reviews

risks.title: AI Model Producing Biased Credit Decisions
risks.description: A machine learning model used in credit adjudication could produce systematically biased outcomes, exposing the institution to regulatory action under OSFI E-23 and reputational harm.
controls: Independent model validation performed before production deployment
Bias and fairness testing across protected demographic segments
Human-in-the-loop review for edge cases flagged by the model
Model performance monitoring dashboard reviewed monthly by model risk team
